description: Complete database schema for the learning platform. Contains all tables covering learner profiles, learning tracks, paths, courses, modules, content, capabilities, artifacts, submissions, evaluations, and XP events.

tables.description: Main learner/student information table. Capability scores and skill recommendations live in learner_capabilities table.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique learner identifier

fields.name: student_id
fields.description: Student ID from students table

fields.name: grade_level
fields.description: Grade level (ug=undergraduate, pg=postgraduate, professional=working professional)

fields.name: skill_level
fields.description: Overall skill level derived from assessment

fields.name: is_active
fields.description: Whether learner account is active or not

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: Record creation timestamp

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: Last update timestamp

indexes.name: idx_learner_student_id
indexes.description: Fast lookup by UUID (login, enrollment)

indexes.name: idx_learner_skill_level
indexes.description: Group/filter by skill tier (beginner, foundation, intermediate, advanced, expert)

indexes.name: idx_learner_grade_level
indexes.description: Filter by academic level (ug, pg, professional)

relationships.description: A learner profile can have multiple recommended learning tracks
All capability scores and recommendations for this learner live in learner_capabilities

tables.description: Learning track recommendations mapped to each learner profile

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique learning track identifier

fields.name: learner_id
fields.description: Learner identifier linked to learner profile

fields.name: assessment_id
fields.description: Assessment identifier that generated this track recommendation

fields.name: fit
fields.description: Fit score label for track relevance

fields.name: track
fields.description: Recommended learning track name

fields.name: match_score
fields.description: Numeric match score for recommendation quality

fields.name: topics
fields.description: Core topics covered in this learning track
fields.example: Node.js/Python, REST APIs, Database design, Authentication

fields.name: duration
fields.description: Estimated completion time

fields.name: why_it_fits
fields.description: Summary justification for why this track fits the learner
fields.example: The student's logical abilities and systematic thinking make backend engineering an ideal career path for building scalable systems.

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: Record creation timestamp

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: Last update timestamp

indexes.name: idx_learning_tracks_learner_id
indexes.description: Get all recommendation tracks for a learner

indexes.name: idx_learning_tracks_fit
indexes.description: Filter by fit quality (High, Medium, Low)

indexes.name: idx_learning_tracks_match_score
indexes.description: Sort recommendations by quality score

indexes.name: idx_learning_tracks_assessment_id
indexes.description: Link track to assessment that generated it

indexes.name: idx_learning_tracks_learner_fit
indexes.description: Get High/Medium/Low tracks for specific learner

relationships.description: Each learning track recommendation belongs to one learner profile
Each learning track has one mapped evidence record

learning_tracks.topics: Node.js/Python, REST APIs, Database design, Authentication
learning_tracks.why_it_fits: The student's logical abilities and systematic thinking make backend engineering an ideal career path for building scalable systems.

learning_tracks.topics: Logging, Monitoring, Error handling, Performance optimization
learning_tracks.why_it_fits: The student's problem-solving capability and emerging system design skills prepare them to handle production incidents and maintain reliable backend services.

learning_tracks.topics: Supervised/Unsupervised Learning, Model evaluation, Feature engineering, Experiment tracking

learning_tracks.topics: ETL design, Python data workflows, Statistical inference, A/B testing
learning_tracks.why_it_fits: The student's blend of data engineering progress and statistical foundation makes this track ideal for building end-to-end, decision-focused data products.

tables.description: Personalized learning path for each learner and their chosen track. Course list, module progress, and 6E stage tracking have been moved to dedicated tables.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique learning path identifier

fields.name: learning_track_id
fields.description: Learning track this path was generated from

fields.name: learner_id
fields.description: Learner this path belongs to

fields.name: role_id
fields.description: Role this learning path is targeting. Every path is tied to one industry role.

fields.name: role_readiness_percentage
fields.description: Cached role readiness percentage (0.00–100.00) for the learner's current active role target. Computed from capability gap progress and updated each time a capability level is approved. Null until the learner selects a role and completes at least one assessment.

fields.name: level
fields.description: Difficulty or progression level of the learning path, ranging from 1 (beginner) to 5 (advanced)

fields.name: status
fields.description: Overall path status

fields.name: is_active
fields.description: Whether this is the learner's current active path

fields.name: version_no
fields.description: Version number of this learning path snapshot

fields.name: is_latest
fields.description: Marks whether this is the latest version for the learner

fields.name: started_at
fields.description: When the learner started this path

fields.name: completed_at
fields.description: When the learner completed this path

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: When the learning path was created

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: Last update timestamp

indexes.name: idx_learning_paths_learner_track
indexes.description: One path per learner per track (prevent duplicates)

indexes.name: idx_learning_paths_learner_is_active
indexes.description: Get active learning path for a learner (usually 1)

indexes.name: idx_learning_paths_status
indexes.description: Filter paths by progress status (in_progress, completed, paused)

indexes.name: idx_learning_paths_learner_status
indexes.description: Get learner's completed or in-progress paths

indexes.name: idx_learning_paths_learner_latest
indexes.description: Get latest learning path version for a learner

indexes.name: idx_learning_paths_role_id
indexes.description: Filter all learning paths targeting a specific role

indexes.name: idx_learning_paths_level
indexes.description: Filter learning paths by difficulty level (1–5)

relationships.description: Each learning path targets one industry role
All courses assigned in this path
All module-level progress records under this path

tables.description: Course catalog. Each course covers exactly one capability and moves the learner from one level to the next (from_level â†’ to_level). This is the core rule: one capability + one level transition = one course. The recommendation engine uses capability_id + from_level to match a course to a learner gap.

fields.name: course_id
fields.description: Unique course identifier

fields.name: course_code
fields.description: Human-readable course code

fields.name: capability_id
fields.description: The single capability this course develops. Every course targets exactly one capability.

fields.name: from_level
fields.description: The learner's capability level before taking this course. The recommendation engine matches this against skill_gap_capabilities.current_level.

fields.name: to_level
fields.description: The capability level the learner reaches upon course completion and artifact approval. Always from_level + 1.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Course title

fields.name: description
fields.description: Course summary
fields.example: Learn how to debug and monitor backend systems for better performance and stability.

fields.name: thumbnail_url
fields.description: Course thumbnail image URL

fields.name: job_pathway_role
fields.description: Primary job role this course supports

fields.name: domain_id
fields.description: Domain classification for this course

fields.name: duration
fields.description: Estimated duration to complete

fields.name: difficulty_level
fields.description: Human-readable difficulty label. Must be consistent with from_level.

fields.name: certification_value
fields.description: Primary certification outcome

fields.name: is_active
fields.description: Whether the course is active in catalog

fields.name: version_no
fields.description: Content version number

fields.name: is_published
fields.description: Whether this course is published for learner access

constraints.name: chk_level_transition
constraints.description: A course always advances exactly one level. from_level 0 â†’ to_level 1, from_level 1 â†’ to_level 2, etc.

constraints.name: chk_from_level_max
constraints.description: from_level cannot be 4 â€” there is no level above L4 to advance to.

unique_constraints.name: uq_course_capability_level
unique_constraints.description: Exactly one course per capability per level transition. The recommendation engine relies on this to return exactly one course per gap.

indexes.name: idx_courses_capability_from_level
indexes.description: Primary lookup for the recommendation engine: given a capability gap, find the one course to recommend

indexes.name: idx_courses_role
indexes.description: Find all courses for a specific role

relationships.description: Each course targets exactly one capability
One course has multiple modules delivered via the 6E framework

notes: Recommendation query: SELECT course_id FROM courses WHERE capability_id = :cap AND from_level = :current_level AND is_active = true AND is_published = true LIMIT 1
If gap > 1 (e.g. current_level=0, required_level=3), the engine recommends 3 courses in sequence: L0â†’L1, then L1â†’L2, then L2â†’L3. Only the first unlocks immediately.
The unique constraint on (capability_id, from_level) guarantees the engine always returns exactly one course.

sample_records.title: Debugging & Observability for Backend Systems
sample_records.description: Learn how to debug and monitor backend systems for better performance and stability.

sample_records.title: Advanced Observability Patterns
sample_records.description: Build production-grade observability pipelines with distributed tracing and alert design.

tables.description: Course modules with evaluation, continuity metadata, and linked 6E flow in separate table

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique module identifier

fields.name: course_id
fields.description: Course identifier linked to courses table

fields.name: module_no
fields.description: Module sequence number

fields.name: title
fields.description: Module title

fields.name: description
fields.description: Module summary
fields.example: Learn how to investigate and debug system failures in production environments.

fields.name: engineering_workflow_stage
fields.description: Stage in engineering incident workflow

fields.name: module_specific_problem_statement
fields.description: Specific real-world problem for the module
fields.example: Production API latency increases during peak traffic.

fields.name: pressure_points
fields.description: Industry pressure points in this module
fields.example: Engineers rely on print statements and cannot locate failure source.

fields.name: student_confusion
fields.description: Common student confusion
fields.example: How backend systems fail under load.

fields.name: learning_content
fields.description: Concept-level learning content for module understanding and application

structure.major_concepts: Core concepts covered in this module
structure.implementation_context_when_to_use: Context or scenarios where concepts should be applied
structure.reason_behind_concept: Why the concept matters in real-world engineering

fields.name: support
fields.description: AI support and module continuity

structure.module_continuity_link: How this module feeds next module

fields.name: knowledge
fields.description: Technical and academic refresher mapping

fields.name: tools
fields.description: Tool information for module execution

fields.name: version_no
fields.description: Content version number for this module. Increment on updates.

fields.name: is_published
fields.description: Whether this module version is published and visible to learners.

fields.name: is_active
fields.description: Whether module is active

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: Record creation timestamp

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: Last update timestamp

indexes.name: idx_modules_course_id
indexes.description: Get all modules for a course

indexes.name: idx_modules_course_module_no
indexes.description: Get modules in sequential order for a course

indexes.name: idx_modules_engineering_workflow_stage
indexes.description: Filter by workflow stage (System Investigation, Signal Analysis, etc)

indexes.name: idx_modules_is_active
indexes.description: Show only active modules

relationships.description: Each module belongs to one course
One module can have multiple content pieces
One module can have progress records for many learners

modules.title: System Failure Investigation
modules.description: Learn how to investigate and debug system failures in production environments.
modules.module_specific_problem_statement: Production API latency increases during peak traffic.
modules.pressure_points: Engineers rely on print statements and cannot locate failure source.
modules.student_confusion: How backend systems fail under load.

learning_content.major_concepts: System architecture, request lifecycle, observability overview

modules.description: Use breakpoints and runtime inspection to isolate backend failures.

modules.module_specific_problem_statement: Engineers fix symptoms rather than root cause.

support.module_continuity_link: RCA report drives final resolution and validation in Module 6.

modules.description: Execute end-to-end incident handling from investigation to validated fix and communication.

tables.description: Maps a module to its 6E stages. One row per module per stage. Acts as the stage container; actual content lives in 6e_content.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for this module-stage record

fields.name: module_id
fields.description: Module this stage record belongs to

fields.name: stage_name
fields.description: 6E stage this record represents for the module

fields.name: stage_order
fields.description: Fixed display order of the stage within the module. 1=engage, 2=explore, 3=explain, 4=express, 5=empower, 6=evolve

fields.name: is_active
fields.description: Whether this stage is active and visible to learners

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: Record creation timestamp

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: Last update timestamp

unique_constraints.name: uq_modules_content_module_stage
unique_constraints.description: Each module can have only one record per 6E stage

indexes.name: idx_modules_content_module_id
indexes.description: Get all stage records for a module

indexes.name: idx_modules_content_stage_name
indexes.description: Filter by 6E stage across all modules

indexes.name: idx_modules_content_module_stage_order
indexes.description: Fetch stages in sequence for a module

relationships.description: Each stage record belongs to one module
One stage record can have multiple content pieces

tables.description: Per-learner capability scores, gap analysis, and role readiness data. One row per learner + assessment + learning path + role + capability. This is the single source of truth for the gap engine â€” current level, required level, gap, and course recommendation priority all live here. skill_gap_capabilities has been merged into this table.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique record identifier

fields.name: learner_id
fields.description: Learner this capability record belongs to

fields.name: assessment_id
fields.description: Assessment that generated this capability score

fields.name: learning_path_id
fields.description: The learning path this capability record is scoped to. Replaces learning_track_id â€” path is the actionable unit a learner works through.

fields.name: role_id
fields.description: The role this capability is being assessed against. Each learning path targets one role.

fields.name: capability_id
fields.description: The specific capability being assessed. References capabilities_master.id.

fields.name: current_score
fields.description: Raw capability score 0â€“100 derived from assessment

fields.name: current_level
fields.description: Learner's current capability level on the L0â€“L4 scale derived from assessment. L0=no exposure, L1=awareness, L2=working knowledge, L3=applied skill, L4=mastery.

fields.name: required_level
fields.description: Minimum capability level required by the role. Sourced from role_capability_map.required_level at the time the learning path was generated.

fields.name: gap
fields.description: Computed gap = required_level - current_level. Positive means learner needs improvement. Zero or negative means no gap.

fields.name: has_gap
fields.description: True if gap > 0. Used by the recommendation engine to find which capabilities need courses assigned.

fields.name: gap_score
fields.description: Gap expressed as a 0â€“100 score for path prioritization and ranking. Derived from current_score vs target score.

fields.name: priority_band
fields.description: Recommendation priority band. A = highest priority gap, B = secondary gap, null = no gap or not yet prioritized.

fields.name: why_needed
fields.description: Why this capability is needed for the learner's target role. Populated for priority A and B rows.
fields.example: Monitor production systems and diagnose runtime issues

fields.name: how_to_build
fields.description: How the learner should build this capability. Populated for priority A and B rows.
fields.example: Complete observability course with logging, debugging, and monitoring tools

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: Record creation timestamp

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: Last update timestamp

constraints.name: chk_current_level_range
constraints.description: current_level must be on the L0â€“L4 scale

constraints.name: chk_required_level_range
constraints.description: required_level must be on the L0â€“L4 scale

constraints.name: chk_gap_computed
constraints.description: gap must always equal required_level - current_level

constraints.name: chk_has_gap_consistent
constraints.rule: (gap > 0 AND has_gap = true) OR (gap <= 0 AND has_gap = false)
constraints.description: has_gap must be true when gap > 0 and false otherwise

indexes.name: idx_learner_capabilities_unique
indexes.description: One capability record per learner per assessment per path per role â€” prevents duplicates

indexes.name: idx_learner_capabilities_learner_path_role
indexes.description: Fetch all capabilities for a learner scoped to a specific path and role

indexes.name: idx_learner_capabilities_has_gap
indexes.description: Fetch all capabilities where learner has a gap â€” primary input to course recommendation engine

indexes.name: idx_learner_capabilities_priority_band
indexes.description: Fetch all priority A or B capabilities for a learner

indexes.name: idx_learner_capabilities_role_capability
indexes.description: Look up required level for a capability within a role

indexes.name: idx_learner_capabilities_learner_assessment
indexes.description: Fetch all capability scores generated from a specific assessment

relationships.description: Each capability record belongs to one learner
Each capability record is generated from one assessment
Each capability record is scoped to one learning path
Each capability record is assessed against one role
Each record references a master capability definition
required_level is sourced from role_capability_map at path generation time

sample_records.how_to_build: Complete observability course with logging, debugging, and monitoring tools
Learn REST/GraphQL principles and database indexing strategies
Master unit testing, integration testing, and CI/CD practices

sample_records.why_needed: Build predictive models for business insights
sample_records.how_to_build: Complete ML engineering courses with real-world projects
Learn ETL frameworks and distributed computing

sample_records.why_needed: Validate data-driven decisions with statistical rigor
sample_records.how_to_build: Study advanced statistics and experimental design

tables.description: Ordered course assignments within a learning path. One row per course per path. Each row represents one level transition for one capability gap. Courses are unlocked sequentially — the first course for each gap is immediately available, subsequent courses for the same gap unlock only after the previous course artifact is approved.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique record identifier

fields.name: learning_path_id
fields.description: Learning path this course belongs to

fields.name: course_id
fields.description: Course from the course catalog

fields.name: capability_id
fields.description: The capability this course addresses. Denormalized from courses.capability_id for fast gap-to-course tracing.

fields.name: from_level
fields.description: Capability level the learner is at when this course is assigned. Denormalized from courses.from_level.

fields.name: to_level
fields.description: Capability level the learner reaches when this course artifact is approved. Denormalized from courses.to_level.

fields.name: sequence_no
fields.description: Overall display order of this course within the full learning path (1-based across all capabilities)

fields.name: capability_sequence_no
fields.description: Order of this course within the same capability gap. e.g. for a gap of 3, this is 1, 2, or 3.

fields.name: is_locked
fields.description: Whether this course is currently locked for the learner. The first course per capability gap starts unlocked. Subsequent courses start locked and unlock when the previous course artifact is approved.

fields.name: unlocks_after_submission_id
fields.description: The submission that must reach approved status to unlock this course. Null for the first course in each capability gap — those are immediately available.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Learner's current status for this course

fields.name: completion_percentage
fields.description: Course completion percentage 0-100 based on module progress

fields.name: started_at
fields.description: When the learner started this course

fields.name: completed_at
fields.description: When the learner completed this course (all artifacts approved)

constraints.name: chk_first_course_not_locked
constraints.rule: capability_sequence_no > 1 OR (capability_sequence_no = 1 AND unlocks_after_submission_id IS NULL)
constraints.description: The first course in each capability sequence must have no unlock dependency.

constraints.name: chk_subsequent_course_has_unlock
constraints.rule: capability_sequence_no = 1 OR unlocks_after_submission_id IS NOT NULL
constraints.description: Any course beyond the first in a capability sequence must reference the submission that unlocks it.

unique_constraints.name: uq_lpc_path_capability_level
unique_constraints.description: One level transition per capability per path — prevents duplicate course assignment for the same gap step

indexes.name: idx_lpc_capability_id
indexes.description: Find all courses assigned for a given capability in a path

indexes.name: idx_lpc_is_locked
indexes.description: Fast fetch of all unlocked courses in a path for dashboard display

indexes.name: idx_lpc_unlocks_after
indexes.description: On submission approval, find which course to unlock next

relationships.description: Nullable — only set for courses that require a prior approved submission to unlock

notes: When gap engine runs and finds capability_id=X with gap=2 (current_level=0, required_level=2), it inserts 2 rows: course L0→L1 (is_locked=false, capability_sequence_no=1) and course L1→L2 (is_locked=true, capability_sequence_no=2, unlocks_after_submission_id=NULL until L0→L1 course artifact is submitted).
On artifact approval for L0→L1 course: set is_locked=false on the L1→L2 row, set unlocks_after_submission_id to the approved submission id.
completion_percentage is recomputed from learner_module_progress rows for this course.

tables.description: Module-level progress header per learner per module. Tracks overall module status, 6E stage completion, artifact submission state, and module lock state. One row per learner per module. Modules unlock sequentially — a module is locked until the previous module's final artifact is approved.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique record identifier

fields.name: learner_id
fields.description: Learner this progress record belongs to

fields.name: learning_path_id
fields.description: Learning path context for this progress record

fields.name: course_id
fields.description: Course this module belongs to

fields.name: module_id
fields.description: Module being tracked

fields.name: module_status
fields.description: Overall module completion status. not_started = unlocked but learner has not entered. in_progress = learner has opened at least one 6E stage. completed = all 6 stages done and final artifact approved.

fields.name: current_stage
fields.description: The 6E stage the learner is currently active in. Null if not_started. Updated each time a stage row is inserted in learner_6e_stage_progress.

fields.name: stages_completed
fields.description: Count of 6E stages completed so far (0-6). Incremented each time a stage moves to completed in learner_6e_stage_progress.

fields.name: completion_percentage
fields.description: Module completion percentage 0-100. Derived as (stages_completed / 6) * 100.

fields.name: artifact_submitted
fields.description: Whether the learner has submitted the module final artifact at least once

fields.name: artifact_approval_status
fields.description: Current status of the final artifact evaluation. Null until artifact is submitted. Set to approved when evaluation flow completes with approval — this is the trigger that unlocks the next module.

fields.name: last_activity_at
fields.description: Timestamp of most recent learner activity in this module

constraints.name: chk_stages_completed_range
constraints.description: stages_completed must be between 0 and 6

constraints.name: chk_completion_percentage_range
constraints.description: completion_percentage must be 0-100

indexes.name: idx_lmp_learner_module
indexes.description: One progress row per learner per module

indexes.name: idx_lmp_course_learner
indexes.description: Fetch all module progress rows for a learner in a course in order

indexes.name: idx_lmp_artifact_approval
indexes.description: Find modules pending review or recently approved for progression trigger

relationships.description: Each module progress row has up to 6 stage rows

tables.description: One row per learner per 6E stage per module. A row is inserted with status=in_progress when the learner first enters a stage. It is updated to completed when they finish. Absence of a row means the stage has not been reached. Stages unlock in strict sequence: completing stage N triggers insertion of stage N+1. The evolve stage (stage 6) gates the artifact submission — the learner cannot submit the module artifact until evolve is completed.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique record identifier

fields.name: learner_module_progress_id
fields.description: Parent module progress header this stage belongs to

fields.name: learner_id
fields.description: Denormalized for direct queries without joining learner_module_progress

fields.name: module_id
fields.description: Denormalized for direct queries

fields.name: stage_name
fields.description: Which of the 6E stages this row tracks. Stages must be completed in order: engage → explore → explain → express → empower → evolve.

fields.name: stage_order
fields.description: Fixed sequence: 1=engage, 2=explore, 3=explain, 4=express, 5=empower, 6=evolve. When stage_order N is completed, insert stage_order N+1 row as in_progress.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Stage status. in_progress = row inserted when learner first enters this stage. completed = updated when learner finishes. No not_started or locked states — absence of a row means not yet reached.

fields.name: started_at
fields.description: When the learner first opened this stage. Set on insert, never null.

fields.name: completed_at
fields.description: When the learner completed this stage. Null while in_progress.

fields.name: time_spent_seconds
fields.description: Total seconds spent in this stage. Accumulated on each session end.

indexes.name: idx_lsp_learner_module_progress_id
indexes.description: Fetch all stage rows under a module progress header

indexes.name: idx_lsp_learner_module
indexes.description: All stages a learner has touched in a module

indexes.name: idx_lsp_learner_module_stage
indexes.description: One row per learner per module per stage

indexes.name: idx_lsp_stage_status
indexes.description: Stage-level analytics and stuck learner detection

tables.description: Defines practice and final artifacts inside each module, including scoring rules and resubmission rules. Question-level content lives in artifact_questions.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique artifact identifier

fields.name: module_id
fields.description: Module this artifact belongs to

fields.name: artifact_type
fields.description: Whether this artifact is practice-only or final submission

fields.name: total_score
fields.description: Total possible score. For practice artifacts use 0 when score is not applicable.

fields.name: passing_score
fields.description: Minimum score to pass. Typically set only for final artifacts.

fields.name: allow_resubmission
fields.description: If false, only one attempt is allowed regardless of outcome. When false, max_attempts and require_review_unlock are ignored.

fields.name: require_review_unlock
fields.description: If true, evaluation flow must reach a terminal state (returned_for_rework, approved, rejected) before the next attempt is unlocked. Ignored if allow_resubmission is false.

fields.name: max_attempts
fields.description: Maximum number of attempts allowed. Null means unlimited. Ignored if allow_resubmission is false.

fields.name: is_active
fields.description: Whether artifact is active and visible

constraints.name: chk_resubmission_rules
constraints.rule: allow_resubmission = true OR (max_attempts IS NULL OR max_attempts = 1)
constraints.description: If allow_resubmission is false, max_attempts must be null or 1.

constraints.name: chk_max_attempts_positive
constraints.description: max_attempts must be at least 1 if set.

constraints.name: chk_passing_score_range
constraints.rule: passing_score IS NULL OR (passing_score >= 0 AND passing_score <= total_score)
constraints.description: passing_score cannot exceed total_score.

tables.description: Template layer for module artifacts. Stores downloadable docs/slides/sheets or external links used by learners.

fields.name: artifact_id

fields.name: file_name
fields.description: Display name of template file

fields.name: file_url
fields.description: Storage/CDN URL or external link

fields.name: file_type
fields.description: Template file category

fields.name: version
fields.description: Template version number

fields.name: is_downloadable
fields.description: Whether learner can download the file

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: Record creation timestamp

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: Last update timestamp

indexes.description: Fetch templates attached to one artifact

tables.description: Learner submission records for module artifacts. One row per attempt. Evaluation details live in artifact_evaluation_flows.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique submission identifier

fields.name: artifact_id
fields.description: Artifact being submitted

fields.name: learner_id
fields.description: Learner who submitted

fields.name: learner_module_progress_id
fields.description: Module progress context for this submission

fields.name: attempt_no
fields.description: Submission attempt number for this learner and artifact

fields.name: version_label
fields.description: Human-readable version label derived from attempt_no. e.g. v1, v2, v3

fields.name: is_latest
fields.description: True for the most recent attempt for a learner-artifact pair. Flipped to false when a new attempt is inserted.

fields.name: previous_submission_id
fields.description: Self-referential pointer to the immediately preceding attempt. Null for attempt_no = 1.

fields.name: submission_mode
fields.description: fast = learner skipped directly to submit. normal = went step-by-step through module content first.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Current lifecycle state of the submission attempt

fields.name: evaluation_id
fields.description: Links to the evaluation flow for this attempt. Null until evaluation is initiated.

fields.name: submitted_at
fields.description: When the learner submitted this attempt

fields.name: sealed_at
fields.description: Timestamp when this attempt was locked and can no longer be edited. Null while status is draft.

constraints.name: chk_previous_submission_first_attempt
constraints.rule: attempt_no = 1 AND previous_submission_id IS NULL OR attempt_no > 1 AND previous_submission_id IS NOT NULL
constraints.description: First attempt must have no previous_submission_id. All subsequent attempts must have one.

constraints.name: chk_version_label_format
constraints.description: version_label must always match attempt_no. v1 for attempt 1, v2 for attempt 2, and so on.

indexes.name: idx_as_artifact_learner_attempt
indexes.description: Enforces unique attempt number per learner and artifact

indexes.name: idx_as_latest
indexes.description: Fast fetch of latest attempt per learner-artifact pair

indexes.name: idx_as_status
indexes.description: Filter submissions by lifecycle state

indexes.name: idx_as_evaluation_id
indexes.description: Join to evaluation flow records

indexes.name: idx_as_previous_submission_id
indexes.description: Walk version chain backwards from any attempt

relationships.description: One submission has up to 3 evaluation stage rows
Links each attempt to its predecessor forming the version chain

tables.description: Master capability reference table. Each row is one unique capability entry sourced directly from the client's Role L1 Gap Model. Fields map 1:1 to client columns. The capability_id is not unique alone â€” the same ID groups multiple named capabilities under one library domain.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Surrogate UUID primary key

fields.name: capability_id
fields.description: Client canonical ID from Role L1 Gap Model. e.g. SE-CAP-01. Not unique alone.

fields.name: capability_library
fields.description: Capability (Library) column from client model. Groups capabilities into a domain area.

fields.name: capability
fields.description: Capability column from client model. The specific named capability.

fields.name: role_id
fields.description: Role this capability belongs to. Every capability is tied to one industry role.

fields.name: domain_id
fields.description: Denormalized FK to domains. Derived from roles.domain_id for fast filtering without joins. Must stay consistent with role's domain.

fields.name: industry_outcome
fields.description: Industry Outcome column from client model.
fields.example: Build maintainable backend services that implement business logic correctly.

fields.name: academic_coverage
fields.description: Academic Coverage column from client model.
fields.example: Programming, Object Oriented Programming, Data Structures

fields.name: correct_academic_subject
fields.description: Correct Academic Subject column from client model.

fields.name: major_topics_refresher
fields.description: Major Topics (Refresher) column from client model.
fields.example: Variables, Data Types, Operators, Control Flow, Functions

fields.name: capability_gap
fields.description: Capability Gap column from client model.
fields.example: Graduates can write functional code but struggle to structure backend services.

fields.name: skill_gap_learning_outcomes
fields.description: Skill Gap (Learning Outcomes) column from client model.
fields.example: Design modular service structure; separate controllers, services, and data layers.

fields.name: gap_review_status
fields.description: Gap Review Status column from client model.

fields.name: micro_gap_examples
fields.description: Micro Gap Examples column from client model.
fields.example: Hardcoded values; poor naming conventions; missing validation.

fields.name: is_active
fields.description: Whether this capability is active in the system.

unique_constraints.name: uq_capabilities_master_id_capability
unique_constraints.description: capability_id + capability name must be unique

indexes.name: idx_capabilities_master_role_id
indexes.description: Get all capabilities under a given role

indexes.description: Get all capabilities under a given domain (denormalized fast lookup)

indexes.name: idx_capabilities_master_role_domain
indexes.description: Filter capabilities by role and domain together

indexes.name: idx_capabilities_master_capability_id
indexes.description: Group all named capabilities under a given capability_id

indexes.description: Filter by review status

relationships.description: Each capability belongs to one role
Each capability belongs to one domain (denormalized from role for fast filtering)
One master capability maps to many learner capability records

tables.note: domain_id is intentionally denormalized from roles.domain_id to avoid joins when filtering capabilities by domain. It must always stay consistent with the role's domain. Sample records below are a representative subset. Full seed data contains ~180 unique capabilities across 240 source rows from Role_L1_Gap_Model.json.

sample_records.industry_outcome: Build maintainable backend services that implement business logic correctly and follow team coding standards.
sample_records.academic_coverage: Programming, Object Oriented Programming, Data Structures
sample_records.major_topics_refresher: Variables, Data Types, Operators, Control Flow, Functions, Arrays/Collections, Object-Oriented Programming, Data Structures, Error Handling, Basic Algorithms
sample_records.capability_gap: Graduates can write functional code but struggle to structure backend services suitable for production environments.
sample_records.skill_gap_learning_outcomes: Design modular service structure; separate controllers, services, and data layers; implement validation and error handling; apply coding standards; refactor code based on review feedback.
sample_records.micro_gap_examples: Hardcoded values; poor naming conventions; missing validation; duplicate logic across modules.

sample_records.industry_outcome: Build frontend and backend code components that work together to deliver usable application features.
sample_records.major_topics_refresher: Variables, Data Types, Operators, Control Flow, Functions, Arrays/Collections, Object-Oriented Programming, Data Structures, Error Handling, Basic Algorithms
sample_records.capability_gap: Students understand programming concepts but struggle to implement business logic across both frontend and backend layers.
sample_records.skill_gap_learning_outcomes: Develop feature logic across UI and service layers; structure reusable code; connect interface actions to business rules.
sample_records.micro_gap_examples: Duplicated logic across layers; fragile feature implementation; weak code organization.

sample_records.industry_outcome: Develop REST APIs that expose application functionality and follow consistent request/response patterns.
sample_records.major_topics_refresher: OSI Model, TCP/IP, HTTP/HTTPS, DNS, Routing, IP Addressing, Network Protocols, Clientâ€“Server Communication
sample_records.capability_gap: Students understand HTTP basics but cannot design production-ready API endpoints or manage API contracts.
sample_records.skill_gap_learning_outcomes: Design REST endpoints using proper resources and verbs; return correct HTTP status codes; implement request validation; structure response payloads; implement pagination and filtering.
sample_records.micro_gap_examples: Always returning HTTP 200; inconsistent API responses; unclear endpoint naming; missing validation.

sample_records.industry_outcome: Diagnose and resolve runtime issues using logs, debugging tools, and systematic troubleshooting.
sample_records.major_topics_refresher: Processes, Threads, Memory Management, File Systems, Scheduling, System Calls, Concurrency, Deadlocks
sample_records.capability_gap: Students debug small programs but lack structured approaches to diagnosing production issues.
sample_records.skill_gap_learning_outcomes: Interpret stack traces; use debuggers effectively; implement structured logging; reproduce bugs through test cases; isolate root causes; write bug reports.
sample_records.micro_gap_examples: Using print statements instead of logs; inability to reproduce bugs; fixing symptoms instead of root cause.

sample_records.industry_outcome: Deploy applications to runtime environments using standard deployment processes.
sample_records.major_topics_refresher: Processes, Threads, Memory Management, File Systems, Scheduling, System Calls, Concurrency, Deadlocks
sample_records.capability_gap: Students understand programs but lack experience deploying applications into real runtime environments.
sample_records.skill_gap_learning_outcomes: Package applications for deployment; run applications in server environments; understand environment variables and configuration.
sample_records.micro_gap_examples: Hardcoded configuration; inability to run applications outside local machine.

tables.description: Tracks the full AI â†’ Staff â†’ Industry evaluation pipeline for a submission. One row per stage per submission. When the final stage reaches an approved decision, the progression engine fires: learner_capabilities.current_level is incremented, the next module or course is unlocked, and XP is awarded.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique evaluation flow record identifier

fields.name: submission_id
fields.description: The submission attempt this stage belongs to

fields.name: stage
fields.description: Which evaluator stage this row represents

fields.name: stage_order
fields.description: Execution order. ai=1, staff=2, industry=3

fields.name: status
fields.description: Lifecycle state of this stage

fields.name: evaluated_by
fields.description: Evaluator user ID. NULL for AI stage, required for staff and industry stages.

fields.name: score
fields.description: Score awarded at this stage (0-100). Null until stage completes.

fields.name: feedback
fields.description: Reviewer comments for this stage
fields.example: Strong evidence mapping. Improve hypothesis prioritization.

fields.name: improvements
fields.description: Improvement points flagged at this stage
fields.example: Add trace correlation IDs and rank hypotheses by confidence.

fields.name: decision
fields.description: Stage outcome. escalate = move to next evaluator stage. return = send back to learner for rework. pass at final stage = triggers progression.

fields.name: completed_at
fields.description: When this stage was completed

fields.name: overall_status
fields.description: Rolled-up flow status across all stages. When this reaches approved, the progression engine fires.

fields.name: is_current_stage
fields.description: True for the currently active stage. Only one row per submission can be true at a time.

constraints.name: chk_evaluated_by_required
constraints.rule: stage = 'ai' AND evaluated_by IS NULL OR stage IN ('staff', 'industry') AND evaluated_by IS NOT NULL
constraints.description: AI stage must have no evaluator. Staff and industry stages must have one.

constraints.name: chk_score_range
constraints.rule: score IS NULL OR (score >= 0 AND score <= 100)
constraints.description: Score must be 0-100 if provided.

constraints.name: chk_progression_only_on_approval
constraints.description: progression_triggered can only be true when overall_status is approved.

indexes.name: idx_aef_submission_id
indexes.description: Fetch all stage rows for a submission

indexes.name: idx_aef_submission_stage
indexes.description: One row per stage per submission

indexes.name: idx_aef_current_stage
indexes.description: Fast fetch of the active stage for a submission

indexes.name: idx_aef_evaluated_by
indexes.description: Fetch all stages assigned to a specific evaluator

indexes.name: idx_aef_overall_status
indexes.description: Filter flows by overall status

indexes.name: idx_aef_approved_not_triggered
indexes.description: Find approved flows where progression has not yet fired â€” used by the progression job as a safety net

relationships.description: Nullable â€” only for staff and industry stages

progression_trigger_logic.description: What happens when overall_status is set to approved
steps: 1. Find the submission: SELECT artifact_id, learner_id, learner_module_progress_id FROM artifact_submissions WHERE id = :submission_id
2. Find the course for this artifact: SELECT course_id FROM modules JOIN module_artifacts ON modules.id = module_artifacts.module_id WHERE module_artifacts.id = :artifact_id
3. Find the course's capability transition: SELECT capability_id, to_level FROM courses WHERE course_id = :course_id

4. Update learner capability level: UPDATE learner_capabilities SET current_level = :to_level, level_label = :to_level_label, updated_at = NOW() WHERE learner_id = :learner_id AND capability_id = :capability_id

5. Update skill gap: UPDATE learner_capabilities SET current_level = :to_level, gap = required_level - :to_level, has_gap = (required_level > :to_level) WHERE learner_id = :learner_id AND capability_id = :capability_id
6. Update module progress: UPDATE learner_module_progress SET artifact_approval_status = 'approved', module_status = 'completed' WHERE id = :learner_module_progress_id
7. Unlock next module: UPDATE learner_module_progress SET is_locked = false WHERE learner_id = :learner_id AND course_id = :course_id AND module_id = (next module_no) AND is_locked = true

8. If all modules in course are completed: UPDATE learning_path_courses SET status='completed', completed_at=NOW(). Then unlock next course in the capability gap sequence by setting is_locked=false on the next learning_path_courses row.
9. Award XP: INSERT INTO xp_events (learner_id, source_type, source_id, xp_awarded, earned_at) VALUES (:learner_id, 'artifact_approved', :submission_id, :xp_amount, NOW())
10. Mark progression as fired: UPDATE artifact_evaluation_flows SET progression_triggered = true WHERE id = :aef_id

sample_records.feedback: Clear incident context. Add stronger evidence correlation.
sample_records.improvements: Strengthen evidence chain with trace IDs.

tables.description: Individual questions or tasks within a module artifact. One artifact can have multiple questions each with their own title, description, instructions and submission requirement.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique question identifier

fields.name: artifact_id
fields.description: Artifact this question belongs to

fields.name: order
fields.description: Display order of this question within the artifact. Starts at 1.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Question title shown to learner

fields.name: description
fields.description: What the learner needs to complete for this question
fields.example: Map the incident timeline, affected services, and likely failure points.

fields.name: instructions
fields.description: Detailed task steps for this question
fields.example: 1) Review incident brief. 2) Mark signal sources. 3) Build failure map.

fields.name: submission_required
fields.description: Whether this specific question requires a submission from the learner.

fields.name: is_active
fields.description: Whether this question is active and visible to learners.

constraints.name: chk_order_positive
constraints.description: Order must start at 1 or above.

indexes.description: Enforce unique order per artifact and drive display sequence

indexes.description: Fetch only active questions for an artifact

relationships.description: Each question belongs to one artifact

sample_records.title: Failure Investigation Map
sample_records.description: Create a first-pass incident map showing service boundaries and symptom points.
sample_records.instructions: 1) Read incident context. 2) Draw request flow. 3) Mark weak signals.

sample_records.title: Hypothesis List
sample_records.description: List at least 3 root-cause hypotheses based on your investigation map.
sample_records.instructions: 1) Review your map. 2) List hypotheses. 3) Rank by likelihood.

sample_records.title: Evidence Mapping
sample_records.description: Map log and trace evidence to each root-cause hypothesis.
sample_records.instructions: 1) Pull relevant logs. 2) Attach trace IDs. 3) Map to hypotheses.

sample_records.title: Final Investigation Report
sample_records.description: Submit a structured report with evidence-backed root-cause and next debugging actions.
sample_records.instructions: 1) Refine your map. 2) Prioritize hypotheses. 3) Submit final report.

indexes.description: Fetch all files for a submission attempt

indexes.name: idx_asf_submission_question
indexes.description: Fetch all files for a specific question within an attempt

relationships.description: File belongs to one submission attempt
File answers one specific question

tables.description: Append-only log of every XP-earning action for a learner. One row per event. Total XP is derived by summing xp_awarded for a learner. Events are written by the progression engine when a stage completes, an artifact is approved, or a course is completed.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique XP event identifier

fields.name: learner_id
fields.description: Learner who earned this XP

fields.name: source_type
fields.description: What action earned the XP. stage_completed = one of the 6 stages finished. artifact_approved = final artifact passed evaluation. course_completed = all modules and artifacts in a course approved.

fields.name: source_id
fields.description: ID of the object that triggered this XP event. For stage_completed: learner_6e_stage_progress.id. For artifact_approved: artifact_submissions.id. For course_completed: learning_path_courses.id.

fields.name: xp_awarded
fields.description: XP points awarded for this event. Suggested defaults: stage_completed=10, artifact_approved=50, course_completed=100.

fields.name: capability_id
fields.description: The capability this XP relates to. Populated for artifact_approved and course_completed events to support capability-level XP dashboards.

fields.name: earned_at
fields.description: When this XP was earned

constraints.name: chk_xp_positive
constraints.description: XP awarded must always be a positive integer

indexes.name: idx_xp_learner_id
indexes.description: Fetch all XP events for a learner (sum for total XP)

indexes.name: idx_xp_learner_source_type
indexes.description: Filter XP events by type for a learner

indexes.name: idx_xp_source_id
indexes.description: Check if XP has already been awarded for a given source (idempotency guard)

indexes.name: idx_xp_capability_id
indexes.description: Capability-level XP breakdown for dashboard

indexes.name: idx_xp_earned_at
indexes.description: Time-series XP history for learner progress chart

relationships.description: Nullable â€” only for artifact and course events

xp_rules.note: These are suggested defaults. Adjust per product requirements. Total XP = SUM(xp_awarded) WHERE learner_id = :lid.

tables.idempotency_note: Before inserting an XP event, check SELECT id FROM xp_events WHERE source_id = :source_id AND source_type = :source_type. If a row exists, skip insertion. This prevents double XP on retry.
